I’ve got the relevant paths now. The core change is small: keep draft controllers in a separate active-draft map, keep implementation capacity based only on active/starting implementation runs, and hard-cap the Ready scheduler to one implementation worker even if persisted config says more.

items.text: Split active run tracking into draft and implementation lanes
Update cancellation and scheduler capacity semantics
Add regression coverage for active draft plus queued Ready tickets
Run focused tests, then typecheck/npm test as practical

payload.text: I’m editing `src/main/services/codex/index.ts` first. The goal is to keep behavior identical at the run-event/ticket-state boundary while changing only which internal map each lane uses.

I’m adding the regression in `tests/backend.test.ts` rather than the draft-only suite because the failure is in the backend Ready scheduler. The test will keep a draft unresolved while exercising the implementation queue.
The implementation and regression test are in place. I’m going to run the focused backend/draft tests first so any scheduler or type-shape issue is easier to isolate before broader validation.
